observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to cease the practice
of the reversions to the cash and liabilities accounts for checks which are not
considered as voided, spoiled, or staled checks to fairly present the accounts
balances in the Financial Statements.

2.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to effect the
necessary adjustments/corrections in the books upon verification, and if
necessary, transfer the overdue amounts to the appropriate funds.

3.15 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. recognize depreciation expense periodically for all depreciable assets;
and
b. prepare adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies noted.
3.16 Moreover, we reiterated and the Municipal General Services Officer and the
Disposal and Appraisal Committee (DAC) agreed to:
a. prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties
(IIRUP) and submit a copy of the same to the Audit Team and DAC for
inspection;
b. prepare the Appraisal Review Report on Property for Disposal (ARRPD)
showing the appraised values of the assets and submit a copy of the same
to the Audit Team for review; and
c. convene and agree on the mode of disposal of the items after receiving the
ARRPD duly reviewed by the audit team and recommend the applicable
mode of disposal for Local Chief Executive’s approval and submit these
documents to the Audit Team for evaluation and action thereon prior to
the disposal of unserviceable items.
3.17 Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to
spearhead efforts to comply with the procedural guidelines of COA Circular
2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in order for the Municipal
Government present fairly its financial statements.

4.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. provide sufficient supporting documents for the adjustments taken up in the
books, and henceforth submit the same together as attachments to approved
JEVs monthly; and
b. review and analyze, and if proper, adjust accordingly the unsupported GL
balances totaling ₱2,987,489.17 and the reported variance of ₱91,200.00.

5.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take-up the current
portion of Loans Payable – Domestic under current liabilities to present fairly
the financial statements of the Municipal Government in accordance with the
pertinent provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards 1.

6.13 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the
Due to BIR account;
b. file an application for a tax refund or request for the over-remitted amount
to be credited against future tax liabilities; and
c. henceforth, ensure that proper reconciliation was made to verify
withholding and remittance amounts before filing and payment to the BIR.
Moreover, we recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to remit the
tax deficiencies noted on the taxes withheld from the suppliers for CY 2024.

7.12 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting entries and, henceforth capitalize the borrowing costs to
the related asset account until the completion of the projects financed through
loans:
Construction in Progress xxx
Prior Period Adjustment xxx

8.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the
pertinent provisions of the IPSAS 1 regarding the disclosure of relevant
information in the Notes to FS for these to be fairly presented.

9.7 We recommended and the:
a. Human Resource Management Officer and other officials concerned
agreed to ensure that entries and attachments to the DTRs of the Job Order
personnel are complete and are faithfully reported, including their work
accomplishments. Further, DTRs and accomplishment reports must be
duly approved and/or countersigned by their immediate supervisors.
b. Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the completeness of supporting
documents before claims are processed as provided in COA Circular No.
2012-001.
c. Management agreed to strictly adhere to the policies and guidelines in the
hiring of personnel under contract of services and/or job orders as
provided for in CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 dated June 5, 2002.

10.15 We recommended and the Human Resource Officer agreed to enforce the
pertinent provision of the Omnibus Rules on Leave and CSC-DBM (JC) No.
2-97 in certifying the leave application for leave of employees applying for the
monetization of their leave credits.

10.16 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to process only
transactions on the monetization of leave credits with proper documentation.

11.7 We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to strictly
require the remittance of collections daily or immediately after their
occurrence, and deposit the same daily and intact, pursuant to Sections 29, 30,
and 32 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local
Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

12.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to maintain
Subsidiary Ledgers for Cash – In Local Treasury account as mandated by
Section 114 (2) of PD 1445 to ensure an accurate balance appearing in the
control account.

13.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General
Services Officer both agreed to prepare the necessary supporting documents
for repairs and maintenance of various PPEs owned and/or operated by the
municipal government before repair work is done and before payment of
repair expenses.

14.8 We recommended that the Management initiate the process of
institutionalizing Internal Audit in the Municipal Government by creating
internal audit-related positions, or if not feasible, by transferring and
converting vacant positions.

14.9 The Local Chief Executive, albeit conveying her willingness to implement the
recommendation, explained to the audit team that Management has yet to ascertain
whether the Municipal Government has the adequate fiscal space to fund the
creation of internal audit-related positions.

15.7 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the LSB to
immediately post the CY 2024 SEF utilization reports in at least three (3)
conspicuous public places. We recommend, thereafter, to direct the LSB to
faithfully comply with the full disclosure policy of the DILG in compliance with
Section 6 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 2017-1.

16.14 We recommend and the GAD Focal Point System agreed to:
a. initiate the conduct of gender analysis and thereafter prepare the GAD
Agenda of the Municipal Government based on the identified gender gaps
or issues therein;
b. ensure compliance with the established submission timelines of the GPB
and GAD AR as outlined in COA Circular No. 2014-001; and
c. assign the appropriate Responsibility Center (RC) for GAD to ensure
proper tracking, monitoring, and reporting of gender-related
expenditures, in accordance with the procedures and guidelines set forth
in COA Circular No. 2021-008.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the implementation of PPAs not yet implemented in CY 2022, and due to lack of subsequent planning spearheads the appropriate planning of priority development projects to come up with PPAs that are well-planned and procurement/implementation-ready as mandated by laws and regulations.
observations.reason: Some PPAs implemented under 20% Development Fund were not implemented in CY 2022 due to lack of appropriate planning and procurement/implementation-ready.

observations.recommendation: Create a committee to 1.) evaluate and monitor the status and progress on the implementation of these livelihood projects; 2.) review the provisions of the loan agreement to ensure repayment of the loans granted; 3.) implement contract provisions which have been violated/breached; and 4.) step up measures to improve collection efficiency.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to process the write off of the account.
observations.reason: Management committed to process the write off of the account.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the
provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards
concerning proper recognition and measurement of the accounts affected by
the DBP eGov bank account.

2.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. prepare adjusting entries to correct the double recording of transactions;
b. submit the complete set of ledgers and journals; and
c. present the accurate amount in the Notes to Financial Statements for the
PPE accounts.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify/trace the noted
unreconciled variance between the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due
to Other Funds, immediately effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in
the books upon verification, and if necessary, transfer the overdue amounts to
the appropriate funds.

4.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit the Trust Fund books
to determine the transactions that brought about the Government Equity
balance and effect the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, observe the proper
recording of transactions in the Trust Fund books, and the preparation of its
financial statements.

5.7 We recommended that the Accountant strictly follow the disclosure
requirements in Item 47 of IPSAS 24 and Annex G, Note 33 of COA Circular
2016-004.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the pertinent
provisions of the IPSAS 1 regarding the disclosure of relevant information in
the Notes to FS for these to be fairly presented.

7.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor install, implement, and monitor
a sound system of internal control in the treasury operations in compliance
with the pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume
I, by directing the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the deposit of all collections and
remittances intact, daily, or as practicable;
b. Revenue Collecting Officers to remit all collections to the Municipal
Treasurer daily or not later than the next working day; and
c. Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting additional cash
advances when the previous cash advance has yet to be settled and
liquidated.

8.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as head of the Municipal
Development Council to require:
a. all concerned personnel to closely monitor the utilization and
implementation of priority projects to ensure that the 20% Development
Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality; and
b. the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to institute proper
planning in the identification of specific/detailed priority development
projects and refrain from including lump-sum appropriation in the 20%
Development Fund.

9.5 We recommended that the MDRRMO include all the unexpended prior years’
balances in the LDRRMFIP to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness to
natural and man-made hazards or disasters.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a corrected bank reconciliation statement by reflecting all reconciling items and adjustments to the affected accounts accordingly. We further recommended that proper coordination between Accounting and the Treasurer’s Office should be made to monitor unreleased checks at year-end so that appropriate adjustments could be made in a timely manner.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant regularly establish RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, the same must be reconciled with the records of the Municipal Treasurer at year-end, in adherence to Section 20, Volume I of NGAs Manual of New Government Accounting System for LGUs. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer regularly update the list of delinquent real property taxes as a basis for the recording of receivables in the books and enforce the collection of unpaid delinquent taxes through tax campaigns.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to implement the recommendation in CY 2024

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE): a. direct the MGSO to retrieve all the property records for PPEs that caused the variance in order to reconcile with his records; b. direct the MA to prepare journal entries for the reclassification of unserviceable and/or obsolete properties of computers into expense/Prior Period Adjustment; c. create a Disposal and Appraisal Committee (DAC); d. require the MGSO to prepare a checklist of all unserviceable PPE with complete data in the IIRUP and submit a copy of the same to the Audit Team and DAC for inspection; e. require the DAC to prepare the Appraisal Review Report on Property for appraisal and Disposal (ARRPD) showing the appraised values of the assets and submit a copy of the same to the Audit Team for review; and f. instruct DAC to convene and agree on the mode of disposal of the items after appraisal and receiving the ARRPD duly reviewed by the Audit Team and recommend the applicable mode of disposal for LCE approval and submit these documents to the Audit Team for evaluation and action thereon prior to the disposal of unserviceable items.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate journal entries to correctly classify the liabilities incurred by the Municipality in the Financial Statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the balances of the accounts affected per the herein proposed adjusting journal entries in the appropriate books of the agency.

We recommended that the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant prepare the required LDRRMFIP and the Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Management Fund in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ensure proper monitoring of the PPAs charged to the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balance of the Interest Expense – DBP account against the Amortization Schedule submitted and prepare the necessary adjustments, if applicable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to evaluate and review the existing unutilized funds for proper disposition thereof, and to return the unutilized balances of excess funds to the source agencies in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 and other applicable guidelines.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer: a. Prepare the Job Hiring Memorandum consistent with the requirements under CSC Resolution No. 020790, specifically by excluding prohibited stipulations on the rendition of 40 hours per week and stipulations on the performance of regular function or work also performed by regular personnel; b. Assess its existing organizational structure so that reasonable plantilla personnel will be established in order to fulfill its mandate of providing basic services; and c. Submit to CSC Regional Office having jurisdiction over the Municipality the Job Hiring Memoranda, MOA, Contract of Service for review of its stipulations within 30 days from the execution of the job order, MOA, or contract.

We recommended that the Municipality thru the LDRRMC identify programs/projects/activities that will strengthen the capacity of the LGU for disaster risk reduction and management and ensure the full implementation of the PPAs within the timeframe to optimize the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a corrected book and bank reconciliation statement by reflecting all reconciling items and adjustments to the affected accounts accordingly. Further, we recommended that coordination should be made between the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office to monitor checks that are stale, cancelled, and unreleased so that appropriate adjustments could be made in a timely manner.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, direct the LDRRMO, the MPDC, the Budget Officer, the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant, and all other officials concerned to see to it that unexpended balances of DRRMF should be strictly appropriated for disaster risk reduction and management activities and that specific expenditures items to be charged against these funds should be identified within the appropriation ordinance. We also recommended that the LDRRMFIP should be reviewed and updated annually to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a monthly report on the sources and utilization of DRRMF and the LDRRMO to submit the report on the fund on or before the 15th day of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC to COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to strictly maintain the required reports and records as provided in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate review/examination of the Inventory account.
observations.reason: Reports and records not yet prepared for the said Inventory balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the inventory committee to conduct the physical count and inspection of unserviceable properties and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) to support its disposal considering that aside from those declared as destroyed by typhoon Yolanda.
observations.reason:
Disposal committee is requesting for trainings/seminar as to the procedures of disposal considering that the committee is newly created and such activity may cause controversy.
Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Create a committee to 1.) evaluate and monitor the status and progress on the implementation of these livelihood projects 2.) review the provisions of the loan agreement to ensure repayment of the loans granted, thus violated/breached, and 3.) implement contract provisions which have been violated/breached, and 4.) step up measures to improve collection efficiency
observations.reason: The Loan Committee has a difficulty in collecting the loan because some of the borrowers were already perished.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare a corrected bank reconciliation statement
by reflecting all reconciling items and draw adjustments to the affected accounts
accordingly. Further, we recommended that coordination should be made
between the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office to monitor checks that are stale,
cancelled, and unreleased so that appropriate adjustments could be made in a
timely manner.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor directs the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the misclassified account and to
reflect the depreciation/accumulated depreciation as of December 31, 2021; and
b. provide depreciation expense periodically for all depreciable assets.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare the adjusting entry to correct the entry made in recording the transfer
of fund; and
b. immediately submit the lacking documentary requirements and ensure that
henceforth, the required documents for the transfer of funds are complete before
transfers are made.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive to instruct
the Municipal Accountant to:
a. transfer all unexpended balances of LDRRMF excluding continuing
appropriations to the Trust Fund books under the Trust Liabilities-DRRM
account; and
b. prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the balances of Expenses and Due
from/to Other Funds in the GF and TF books; and Trust Liabilities-MDRRMF
in the TF Books.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant record the
necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the Loans Payable -
Domestic and Loans Receivables – GOCCs and thereafter, record only those loan
proceeds actually received as Loans Payable.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant complies
with the required presentation and disclosure format of PPE in the Notes to FS as
prescribed in Annex G of the COA Circular 2016-004.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instructs the Municipal Accountant to prepare an accounting entry in the General Fund book in order that the unutilized balance be reflected Trust Liabilities- BGCM and to correct the overstatement in the Government Equity account.
Suggested Accounting Entry:
Government Equity ₱47,541.00
Trust Liabilities - GCM ₱47,541.00

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct officers concerned to follow the pertinent laws and regulations affecting the procurements conducted in extra-ordinary cases like COVID19 pandemic, so as to promote transparency and accountability in the conduct of the procurement.
b. Instruct officers concerned to undertake the required posting at the GPPB Online Portal of those posted procurements, continuously.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct each end-user or implementer for each activity to ascertain its necessity and provide documents to support the same in order to clearly monitor the implementation thereof. The “purpose” in the Purchase Request must be properly indicated, describing therein the exact purpose to which the request is made for in order to prevent dubious interpretation thereof and prevent irregularity in the implementation.
b. Remind each officers concerned of the necessity of documentary evidence to support each transaction. And for the above-mentioned transactions, for them to provide supporting documents to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance if warranted.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the officers concerned to affix at all times the actual date of receipt of items received from the supplier in accordance with pertinent regulations in order not to confuse as to the true date of delivery and thus facilitate in assessing whether the supplier could be charged with liquidated damages as expressly provided by law and in the contract.
b. Instruct all its officers to be mindful of any alterations introduced in an official document as it has a corresponding criminal sanction, if proven.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instructs the Petty Cash Custodian to prepare the Petty Cash Vouchers at the time of the release of the cash to the requesting payee and require the latter to accomplish the required boxes in the PCV upon liquidation. This is to prevent misinformation to the Budget Officer who relies on the PCV/Summary of Petty Cash Disbursements.

We recommended that the Accountant be required to submit immediately the lacking disbursement documents and complete relevant supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of accounts and other financial reports as required under Sections 4, 39, 107 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 7.2.1 (a) and (b) of COA Circular 2009-006. We further recommend that Management suspend the payment of the salaries of the responsible employees for the delayed and non-submission of the required reports until these have been complied in pursuance with Section 127 of the same Code.

We recommended that the Accountant be required to submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers with complete relevant supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set for the disbursement and submission of accounts and other financial reports as required under Sections 4, 39, 107 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 7.2.1 (a) and (b) of COA Circular 2009-006.
We further recommend that Management suspend the payment of the salaries of the responsible employees for the delayed and non-submission of the required reports until these have been complied in pursuance with Section 127 of the same Code.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation between the audit team’s list and that of the accounting office files and records of transmittals of cancelled checks.

observations.recommendation: Create a committee to 1.) evaluate and monitor the status and progress on the implementation of these livelihood projects 2.) review provisions of the loan agreement to ensure enforcement and collection 3.) implement contract provisions which have been violated/breached , and 4.) step up measures to improve collection efficiency.
observations.reason: On-going assessment of beneficiaries’ financial status and issuance of demand letter.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal
Accountant and Supply Officer to strictly maintain the required reports and
records as provided in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and
submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate review/examination of the
Inventory account.
1.8 The Management agreed with the recommendation and further commented that
they are now fixing the records and reports as required in the GAM for LGUs.

2.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instructs the Municipal
Accountant to prepare an accounting entry in the General Fund book in order
that the unutilized balance be reflected as Trust Liabilities-BGCM and to
correct the overstatement in the Government Equity account.
Suggested Accounting Entry:
Government Equity ₱47,541.00
Trust Liabilities – BGCM ₱47,541.00
2.10 The Management agreed with the recommendation and further reiterated that they
were confused as to the correct treatment of the fund transfer. They also asserted
that the unutilized balance represents savings in the implementation of the
identified PPAs under BGCM.
2.11 The Team asserted that the incorrect recognition prevented fair presentation in the
financial statements. The team likewise advised the officers to use the remaining
fund for COVID19 related activities so long as the national state of calamity is not
yet lifted.

3.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the inventory
committee to conduct the physical count and inspection of unserviceable
properties, aside from those declared as destroyed by typhoon Yolanda, and
prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP) to support its disposal and derecognition in the books.
3.8 The Management agreed on the recommendations and further asserted that they are
hesitant to proceed with the disposal as they have not yet undergone training on the
same.
3.9 The team advised the Management to refer to the Supply and Property Management
Manual for its guidance in the disposal.

4.15 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct officers concerned to follow the pertinent laws and regulations
affecting procurements conducted in extra-ordinary cases like during
COVID19 pandemic, so as to promote transparency and accountability in
the conduct of the procurement.
b. Instruct officers concerned to undertake the required posting at the GPPB
Online Portal of those procurements undertaken in the implementation of
Bayanihan to Heal as One Act.

5.7 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct each end-user or implementer for each activity to ascertain its
necessity and provide documents to support the same in order to clearly
monitor the implementation thereof. The portion for the “purpose” in the
Purchase Request must be properly indicated, describing therein the exact
purpose to which the request is made for in order to prevent dubious
interpretation thereof and prevent irregularity in the implementation.
b. Remind each officers concerned of the necessity of documentary evidence
to support each transaction. And for the above-mentioned transactions, for
them to provide supporting documents to prevent the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance if warranted.
5.8 The Management commented that the Municipal Health Officer was the designated
over-all in-charge as mandated under the Incident Command System, hence the
Purchase Request was signed by the said officer. Moreover, they asserted that they
will provide those necessary documents to validated the disbursement.

6.8 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the officers concerned to affix at all times the actual date of receipt
of items received from the supplier in accordance with pertinent
regulations in order not to confuse as to the true date of delivery and thus
facilitate in assessing whether the supplier could be charged with
liquidated damages as expressly provided by law and in the contract.
b. Instruct all its officers to be mindful of any alterations introduced in an
official document as it has a corresponding criminal sanction, if proven.
6.9 Management commented that the Delivery Receipt was lacking during the
processing of the disbursement voucher, hence they requested it from the supplier,
on the same date appearing as the system date and time, that is, November 20,
2020; but the document is dated October 23, 2020. They further adduced that they
already noted that there is discrepancy in the dates in the Acceptance and
46
Inspection Report and the Sales Invoice (SI) so they asked the personnel who
picked-up the items from the supplier and it disclosed that it was in October 23,
2020, so the date in the SI was altered to reflect the true date.
6.10 The team reiterated that said alterations cast doubt as to how or why the same was
introduced to the document. Management was advised to be careful in making
changes/alterations. And that any alterations be signed by the officer concerned;
likewise, not to introduce changes by overriding the writings or marking on the
document but rather to draw line through it to show that it was wrong.

7.7 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instructs the Petty Cash Custodian to prepare the Petty Cash Vouchers at the
time of the release of the cash to the requesting payee and require the latter to
accomplish the required boxes in the PCV upon liquidation. This is to prevent
misinformation to the Budget Officer who relies on the PCV/ Summary of
Petty Cash Disbursements for purposes of determining the fund to which said
disbursement is chargeable and thus would result to the charging of
obligations to the correct funds in accordance with the approved budget.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to conform and apply the accounting policies/standards in the presentation of cash in bank balance in the financial statements in order that the financial statements fairly reflect the actual status of the account. Furthermore, for the Municipal Accountant to effect adjustments to reflect the above noted findings.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amount presented in the Financial Statements for the PPE account with that of the corresponding property ledger cards for each item of property, plant and equipment of the LGU in order to present a reliable amount for the said account.
Moreover, to reconcile accounting records with the result of the physical count conducted at year-end and make the necessary adjustments or corrections in order that the financial statements present items of PPE that exist at year-end.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries so as to make a faithful presentation of the actual status of the transaction in the financial statements.
Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officers concerned to adhere to the established accounting system for the receipt, issuance and recording of supplies and property so as to facilitate proper recording in the books and thus produce a financial statement that is reflective of the transactions made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to provide proper cross-referencing of the financial statements to its notes.
Also, to present in the notes the required disclosures necessary for users to know and understand the lump sum amount in each line item in the financial statements in order that the financial statements could serve its purpose. i.e. to provide information to general public users of the financial status and performance of the LGU for the given period.

We recommended to the Head of Procuring Entity require the BAC to conduct proper verification and validation of bidding documents in the post-qualification stage of the procurement process to avoid doubt on the accuracy of the action made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officers the imposition of liquidated damages for the delayed deliveries of the procured items and forfeit the supplier’s performance security as stipulated in GCC clause 23 for the failure of the latter to comply the provisions of the contract agreement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officers concerned to carefully examine all items received in accordance to the particular specifications provided in the contract so that items received will serve the desired result the program aims to achieve.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive prepare a memorandum of agreement with the farmer beneficiaries as provided for in the MOA between the LGU-Balangiga and DA-RF08 in order to properly monitor the responsibilities of the agricultural supplies, materials and equipment issued to the beneficiaries for their efficient and effective usage for sustainable livelihood.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the officers concerned to fast-track the implementation of the remaining unimplemented projects before the two-year duration of the project ends in order that the identified beneficiaries of said projects be able to enjoy the agricultural developments expected from the implementation of the same.
It is further recommended that the LCE enjoins all officers concerned to act in any project either funded locally or through aids from other government agencies, in order to fully utilize the fund in accordance to the purpose for which said projects are aimed for.
observations.reason: (Remaining fund was returned to the DA)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as head of the Local Development Council must ensure the full implementation of the projects and programs under the development fund with the assurance of proper planning and implementation in order to achieve the expected benefits therefrom.
It is further recommended that the LCE enjoins all officers concerned to act in any project either funded locally or through aids from other government agencies, in order to fully utilize the fund in accordance to the purpose for which said projects are aimed for.
observations.reason: (Completed the implementation. Remaining projects have pending resolution of road-right of way issue.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present accurate and reliable data on the Municipal's financial statements as of a given period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer (LDRRMO), to implement the programs, projects and activities on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal officials concerned to conduct procedures that will reconcile all its records for the Property, Plant and Equipment to assure the users of the financial statements on its existence, accuracy and reliability and in order to achieve the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should give priority in implementing projects under the 20% Economic Development Fund to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for its constituents in accordance with Section 287 of Local Government Code and ensure that the Annual Investment Program discloses all the projects/programs/activities for the year in order to give information to the general public for the projects to be implemented during the calendar year of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials concerned to strictly monitor the implementation of the projects as planned and determine the reasons for the delay on the implementation and priority projects under the BuB-LGSF in order to formulate the next steps to be taken by the agency and to achieve the pursuit of attaining the Philippine Development Plan’s goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction and promoting good governance at the local level.
observations.reason: (Project was implemented on areas with no Road Right of Way Issues: (a) Brgy. 06 – Academy; (b) Brgy. 2)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should regularly update the Ledger Cards on the depreciation of the assets to substantiate its accounting, measurement and valuation of the assets presented in the financial statements of the agency and to comply with the standards stated in PPSAS.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant   to   strictly   conform    and    apply    the    accounting
policies/standards in the presentation of cash in  bank  balance  in  the
financial statements  in  order  that  the  financial  statements  fairly
reflect the actual status of  the  said  account.  Furthermore,  for  the
Municipal Accountant to effect adjustments to  reflect  the  above  noted
findings.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the amount presented in the Financial  Statements
for the PPE account with that of the corresponding property ledger  cards
for each item of property, plant and equipment of the  LGU  in  order  to
present a reliable amount for the said account.  Moreover,  to  reconcile
accounting records with the result of the physical  count  conducted  at
year-end and make the necessary adjustments or corrections in order  that
the financial statements present items of PPE that exist at year-end.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary  adjusting  entries  so  as  to  make  a
faithful presentation of the actual status  of  the  transaction  in  the
financial statements.

Moreover, we recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the
officers concerned to adhere to the established accounting system for the
receipt, issuance and  recording  of  supplies  and  property  so  as  to
facilitate proper recording in the books and  thus  produce  a  financial
statement that is reflective of the transactions made by the LGU.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant  to  provide  proper  cross-referencing   of   the   financial
statements to its notes. Also, to  present  in  the  notes  the  required
disclosures necessary for users to  know  and  understand  the  lump  sum
amount in each line item in the financial statements in  order  that  the
financial  statements  could  serve  its  purpose.   i.e.   to   provide
information  to  general  public  users  of  the  financial  status  and
performance of the LGU for the given period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should update the Ledger Cards on the depreciation of the assets to substantiate its accounting, measurement and valuation of the assets accounts presented in the financial statements of the agency and to comply with the standards stated in PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2016
observations.reason: No submission of Property Ledger Cards.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the account balances in the financial statements and
notes to financial statements in order to present fairly the accounts and not to
mislead the users, also present it in comparative form to achieve the
comparability of information over periods.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned
municipal officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No.
2015-008 on the proper accounting, recording and reporting for local road
network to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to
assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to
reflect the correct account balances and present accurate and reliable data on
the Municipal's financial statements as of a given period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to make necessary adjusting journal entries for the depreciation to
reflect the correct amount/value of the property, plant and equipment account
and to present accurate information of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to approve only the valid
work suspensions as recommended in Revised IRR of R.A 9184 and direct the
municipal officials concerned to consider the unfavorable situations in
determining the duration of the projects in order not to hamper the
agriculture-related economic benefits it can give to the constituents. Further,
we also recommend to require the Municipal Engineer to compute the
number of delays in the implementation of projects and apply liquidated
damages as penalty to the contractor.

We recommended that the Accountant be required to submit immediately the
lacking disbursement vouchers with complete relevant supporting documents
and to keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of accounts and
other financial reports as required under Sections 4, 39, 107 and 122 of P.D.
No. 1445 and Sections 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 7.2.1 (a) and (b) of COA Circular 2009-006.
We further recommend that Management suspend the payment of the
salaries of the responsible employees for the delayed and non-submission of
the required reports until these have been complied in pursuance with
Section 127 of the same Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the monthly and quarterly trial balance, financial
statements and its accompanying schedules within the period prescribed by
the above rules and regulations in order to have a timely verification and
analysis of accounts and the immediate submission of the audit results to
management to have a reliable information on the financial condition and
results of operations of the municipality.

We recommend that the municipal official concerned shall follow the proper and approved amortization schedule in the payment of loan, so that excess payment could be avoided and funds can be used for some priority projects, programs and activity of the municipality. Also, ensure that any changes made on the agreement should be in writing to have a legal and binding effect and a proper adjustment of Loans Payable understatement shall be made to reflect the correct balance as of December 31, 2017.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal officials concerned shall strictly follow the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, no additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made to avoid exposure of funds to misuse and/or misappropriation and to ascertain the propriety and validity of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials concerned to conduct procedures that will reconcile all its records for Property, Plant and Equipment to assure the users of the financial statements on its existence, accuracy and reliability of the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2017 1. Prepared CYs 2016, 2015 and 2014 RPCPPE & Property Ledger Cards. 2. Submitted inventory report of PPE and supporting pictures of infrastructure projects. 3. Reconcile variances upon completion of RPCPPE and Property Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: There were six (6) asset accounts remained to be unreconciled. Moreover, there were no Property Ledger Cards submitted for some items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should give priority in implementing projects under the 20% Economic Development Fund to achieve desirable socio-economic outcomes for its constituents in accordance with Section 287 of the Local Government Code and ensure that the Annual Investment Program discloses all the projects/programs/activities for the year in order to give information to the general public for the projects to be implemented during the calendar year of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2017 that the Local Chief Executive should give priority in implementing projects under the 20% Economic Development Fund and ensure disclosure in the AIP.
observations.reason: Four (4) projects remained to be unimplemented as of December 31, 2018, to wit: 1. Construction of Demo Farm Access Road 2. Road Opening – Brgy.1 3. Expansion of BWSS 4. Road Opening – Brgy.1 to 3

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials concerned to ensure that all expenses under the Special Education Fund (SEF) are included in the annual budget duly approved by the Local School Board and in consonance to the above laws and regulation to avoid irregularity of transactions and disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal officials concerned practice the preparation of obligation request for disbursements to have proper control of the fund and to substantiate and verify the existence of available appropriation for the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2017
observations.reason: Disbursements for SEF were submitted first to the Budget Officer for obligation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the needed supporting documents on the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants and usage of government vehicle, and submit the monthly reports to ascertain the authority on the use of government vehicles and to properly determine, monitor and control the monthly fuel consumption. Moreover, the disbursements made should be correctly recognized in the books according to its purpose upon purchase. To correct the said error in recording the transaction, we recommend the following adjusting entry:

Check No. 1049006: Fuel, oil and lubricants inventory 1,056.44 Government equity 1,056.44

Check No. 1049155: Fuel, oil and lubricants inventory 19,411.00 Government equity 19,411.00
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2017
observations.reason: Trip tickets were attached and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials concerned to strictly monitor the implementation of the projects as planned and determine the reasons for the delay on the implementation of the priority projects under the BuB-LGSF in order to formulate the next steps to be taken by the agency and to achieve the pursuit of attaining the Philippine Development Plan’s goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction, and promoting good governance at the local level.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2017 1. Agency offered to purchase for the affected properties by the List of Road Opening Completed and On-going Infrastructure Projects for the project. 2. Agency sought the assistance of the Provincial Prosecutor for the expropriation of those properties. 3. Contractor started the project on pursuit areas with no road right of way problem.
observations.reason: There was still no implementation of the project as verified in the List of all Completed and On-going Infrastructure Projects for CY 2018, however, the agency already filed to the court for the expropriation of the properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor create the Local GAD Focal Point System in order to implement effectively and efficiently the local GAD programs, projects and activities, ensure alignment with the guidelines on the localization of the Magna Carta of Women (MCW) and to address the gender issues and concerns in the locality which are beneficial to both the LGU clientele and the organization. Moreover, prepare and submit approved GAD plan and budget, GAD Accomplishment Report, GAD Code and Database to the DILG for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2016 Issuance of E.O, GAD plan & budget preparation, submission & execution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor comply strictly all the provisions of R.A. 9003 otherwise known as the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to achieve the intended purpose of the law. Moreover, prepare and submit Local Solid Waste Management Plans for approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and make representation with the SB Council for the inclusion in the annual budget Solid Waste Management Programs, Projects and Activities for immediate and proper implementation.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2016 that the Municipal Mayor through its Solid Waste Management Board comply and prepare the Solid Waste Management Plan for approval.
observations.reason: Solid Waste Management Plan is for revision due to some changes made by EMB. Solid Waste Committee.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the GSIS for its inspection, appraisal, and assessment as preparatory to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) coverage.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2013 Submit to GSIS the list of properties for coverage and inspection, appraisal and assessment.
observations.reason: Sec. 5 of R.A 656 otherwise known as the Property Insurance Law states that every government, except a municipal government below first class, is hereby required to insure its properties.

observations.recommendation: Create a committee to 1.) evaluate and monitor the status and progress on the implementation of these livelihood projects 2.) review the provisions of the loan agreement to ensure repayment of the loans granted 3.) implement contract provisions which have been violated/breached and 4.) step up collection measures to improve collection efficiency.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2012 1.) assess beneficiaries’ financial status and issue demand letter. 2.) review the provisions of the loan agreement and monitoring of the status & progress on the implementation of the livelihood projects. 3.) implement contract provisions. 4.) step up collection measures.
observations.reason: Management created a committee committed to evaluation and monitoring and issued Executive Order No. 006 dated February 24, 2017 but beneficiaries’ livelihood projects were still on-going. The amount is still outstanding as of Dec. 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality immediately implement the provisions of the Republic Act No. 9003, otherwise known as the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to achieve the intended purpose of the law. Moreover, prepare and submit Local Solid Waste Management Plans for approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and make representation with the SB Council for the inclusion in the annual budget Solid Waste Management Programs, Projects and Activities for immediate and proper implementation.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Not implemented. Preparation of Solid Waste Management Plan is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality immediately implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Not Implemented. Preparation of Solid Waste Management Plan is on-going.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) for inspection, appraisal, and assessment preparatory to property insurance coverage consistent with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Not Implemented. Management is working on its paper for the application of property insurance with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal official concerned to follow the proper and approved amortization schedule in the payment of loan, so that excess payment could be avoided and funds can be used for some priority projects, programs and activity of the municipality. Also, ensure that any changes made on the agreement should be in writing to have a legal and binding effect and a proper adjustment of Loans Payable shall be made to reflect the correct balance as of December 31, 2017.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, no additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made to avoid exposure of funds for misuse and misappropriation and to ascertain the propriety and validity of the transactions.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal officials concerned to conduct procedures that will reconcile all its records for the Property, Plant and Equipment to assure the users of the financial statements on its existence, accuracy and reliability and in order to achieve the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should give priority in implementing projects under the 20% Economic Development Fund to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes for its constituents in accordance with Section 287 of Local Government Code and ensure that the Annual Investment Program discloses all the projects/programs/activities for the year in order to give information to the general public for the projects to be implemented during the calendar year of the municipality.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials concerned to ensure that all expenses under the Special Education Fund (SEF) are included in the annual budget duly approved by the Local School Board and in consonance to the above laws and regulation to avoid irregularity of transactions and disallowance in audit.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and/or
Disallowance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal budget officer to prepare the obligation request for all disbursements made under the Special Education Fund (SEF) by the agency in order to have a proper control of the fund and to substantiate and verify the existence of available appropriation.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal accountant to strictly comply with the needed supporting documents on the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants and usage of government vehicle, and submit the monthly reports in order to ascertain the authority on the use of government vehicles and to properly determine, monitor and control the monthly fuel consumption. Moreover, the disbursements made should be correctly recognized in the books according to its purpose upon purchase.
To correct the said error in recording the transaction, we recommend the
following adjusting entry:
Check No. 1049006:
Fuel, oil and lubricants inventory 1,056.44
Government equity 1,056.44
Check No. 1049155:
Fuel, oil and lubricants inventory 19,411.00
Government equity 19,411.00

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or
Notice of Disallowance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials concerned to strictly monitor the implementation of the projects as planned and determine the reasons for the delay on the implementation of priority projects under the BuB-LGSF in order to formulate the next steps to be taken by the agency and to achieve the pursuit of attaining the Philippine Development Plan’s goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction, and promoting good governance at the local level.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements for all funds maintained for Cash in Bank in pursuance to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 to 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to present a reliable and accurate balance of the account.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal & Accountant reconcile its records regarding the assets of the agency to achieve completeness of its records and fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Reconciliation of Property, Plant and Equipment Cards & RPCPPEs and Ledger Cards. Accountant Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: The agency submitted reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should update the Ledger Cards on the depreciation of the assets to substantiate its accounting, measurement and valuation of the assets accounts presented in the financial statements of the agency and to comply with the standards stated in PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2016 Property Cards & RPCPPEs and Ledger Cards. Accountant Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: Only partial submission of Property Cards.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Treasurer and all concerned LGU officials and employees submit payrolls, liquidations report for the payment of salaries and wages, allowances, traveling expenses and other MOOE pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive comply with Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to enhance disaster preparedness and reduce disaster risk. Likewise, look into the reasons why the PPAs in the LDRRMFIP were not implemented during the year and review the programmed PPAs to improve the delivery of the LGU services to its constituents. Conduct also regular monitoring and supervision to ensure implementation of the PPAs.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create the Local GAD Focal Point System in order to implement effectively and efficiently the local GAD programs, projects and activities, ensure alignment with the guidelines on the localization of the Magna Carta of Women (MCW) and to address the gender issues and concerns in the locality which are beneficial to both the LGU clientele and the organization. Moreover, prepare and submit approved GAD plan and budget, GAD Accomplishment Report, GAD Code and Database to the DILG for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Issuance of E.O, GAD Focal Point System in order to implement the local GAD programs, submission & execution.
observations.reason: The agency created a Local GAD Focal Point System through E.O 07 dated February 21, 2017 but accomplishment reports were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management strictly comply with Section 3 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 by the DILG and DBM on the utilization of the Development Fund to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of the residents of the municipality. Such will likewise prevent the suspension or disallowance of pertinent transactions on future audits.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Implemented.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the AAR, and create a committee that will focus on the listing of the damaged properties and support them with all necessary pictures and documents and request relief from accountability as required per COA Circular No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992. Then require the GSO to prepare and update the Property Cards, and the Accounting Department to prepare and update the Real Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards. With these, the proper accounting of the difference of P44,396,771.33 could be undertaken, and that would be the start of conducting the reconciliation of accounting and property records. Henceforth, the periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted, in compliance with Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU will be completely/accurately accounted for and the unserviceable properties could be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Partially implemented. The creation of a committee was implemented as of the CY 2016. But the preparation of Property Cards and reconciliation of Property Cards and Property, Plant & Equipment Ledger Card are still on-going.
observations.reason: The agency implemented the creation of a committee as of CY 2016 but Property Cards and reconciliation are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in the form and manner prescribed in the NGAS Manual for Local; and Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the PPE account ledger balances with that of the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: AAR, immediately implement the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment. The RPCPPE for CY 2014 already submitted. However, totals in this report do not tally with the records of the Accounting.
observations.reason: RPCPPE for CY 2014 submitted but totals do not tally with accounting records.

observations.recommendation: Create a committee to 1.) evaluate and monitor the status and progress on the implementation of these livelihood projects 2.) review the provisions of the loan agreement to ensure repayment of the loans granted 3.) implement contract provisions which have been violated/breached and 4.) step up measures to improve collection efficiency.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Partially implemented. Management created a committee and committed through Executive Order No. 006 dated February 24, 2017 but evaluation & monitoring of the beneficiaries and implementation of the livelihood projects were still on-going.
observations.reason: Evaluation and monitoring of beneficiaries and implementation still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality immediately implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.managementAction: AAR, immediately implement the Municipality Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.reason: Reiterated in part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), for its inspection, appraisal, and assessment and for the preparatory application to property insurance consistent with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
observations.managementAction: AAR, immediately submit the list of insurable properties to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) for its inspection, appraisal, and assessment and for the preparatory application to property insurance.
observations.reason: Management is working on its paper application for the insurance with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: Create a committee to 1.) evaluate and monitor the status and progress on the implementation of these livelihood projects 2.) review the provisions of the loan agreement to ensure repayment of the loans granted 3.) enforce contract provisions which ensure repayment of the loans and 4.) step up and enforce measures to improve collection efficiency of the loans granted.
observations.managementAction: AAR, to monitor the status and progress on the implementation of these livelihood projects and to review the provisions of the loan agreement to ensure repayment of the loans granted.
observations.reason: Management is thinking of solutions to collect outstanding balances because some individuals violated/breached the loan agreement and some loan were already dead.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly
bank reconciliation statements for all funds maintained for Cash in Bank in
pursuance to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 to 3.4 of COA Circular No.
96-011 in order to present a reliable and accurate balance of the account.

We recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal
Accountant reconcile its records regarding the assets of the agency to achieve
completeness of its records and fair presentation of the accounts in the financial
statements. Likewise, always prepare and submit the RPCPPEs, Property Cards
and Ledger Cards to verify the accuracy and reliability of the Property, Plant
and Equipment account.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should regularly update the
Ledger Cards on the depreciation of the assets to substantiate its accounting,
measurement and valuation of the assets accounts presented in the financial
statements of the agency and to comply with the standards stated in PPSAS.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and all concerned LGU officials
and employees submit payrolls, liquidation report of TEVs pursuant to COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommend that the management comply strictly Republic Act No. 10121 and
Section 3 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to enhance and
support disaster preparedness and reduce disaster risk. Likewise, look into the
reasons why the PPAs in the LDRRMFIP were not implemented during the year
and review the programmed PPAs to improve the delivery of the LGU services to
its constituents. Conduct also regular monitoring and supervision to ensure
implementation of the PPAs.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor create the Local GAD Focal Point
System in order to implement effectively and efficiently the local GAD programs,
projects and activities, ensure alignment with the guidelines on the localization of
the Magna Carta of Women (MCW) and to address the gender issues and
concerns in the locality which are beneficial to both the LGU clientele and the
organization.

Moreover, prepare and submit approved GAD plan and budget, GAD
Accomplishment Report, GAD Code and Database to the DILG for review and
evaluation.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor through its Solid Waste Management
Board comply strictly all the provisions of the Republic Act No. 9003 or otherwise
known as the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to achieve the
intended purpose of the law.

Moreover, prepare and submit Local Solid Waste Management Plans for
approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and make
representation with the SB Council for the inclusion in the annual budget Solid
Waste Management Programs, Projects and Activities for immediate and proper
implementation.

We recommend that the management strictly comply with Section 3 of Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 by the DILG and DBM
on the utilization of the Development Fund to achieve desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of the residents of the
municipality. Such will likewise prevent the suspension or disallowance of
pertinent transactions on future audits.

The Local Chief Executive should create a committee that will focus on the listing of damaged properties only support them with all necessary pictures and documents and request relief from accountability as per COA Circular No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992. Then require the GSO to prepare and update the Property Cards, the Real Property Cards and Ledger Cards. With these, the proper accounting of the difference of P44,396,771.33 could be undertaken, and that would be the start of conducting the reconciliation of accounting and property records. Henceforth, the periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted, in compliance with Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU will be completely/accurately accounted for and the unserviceable properties could be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The creation of a committee was the one implemente d as of the CY 2016. But the preparation of property cards and updating of Property, Plant & Equipment Ledger Card and reconciliation of Property Cards and the Accounting Department to prepare and update the Real Property Cards and Ledger Card are still on-going.
observations.reason: The balances presented in the RPCPPEs and Ledger Cards will not reconcile because they already don’t have the records of the properties from the previous years.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the BAC, Secretariat to submit the contracts and the supporting documents which form part of the contract in all future contracts that the municipality may enter into for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: AAR, BAC, Secretariat to submit the contracts and the supporting documents which form part of the contract in all future contracts that the municipality may enter into for review and evaluation.

observations.recommendation: Management, specially the BAC, should plan all procurement of the LGU based on its Annual Procurement Plan and Annual Investment Plan.

Submit immediately the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in the form and manner prescribed in the NGAS Manual for Local; and Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the PPE account ledger balances with the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: AAR, immediately the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) submitted.
observations.reason: RPCPPE for CY 2014 and 2015 already submitted. However, totals in this report do not tally with the records of the Municipal Accounting.
